data.description:
## Why
This is the dependency root of the oracle. The three-valued evidence contract, the fail-open execution harness,
and the optional-extra scaffolding are shared by every engine — they must exist and be stable before Engine A,
T0, or Engine B are built. Getting the contract right here is what makes "one implementation, not two" real.

## What
- The normalized **evidence model**: `resolved | abstained(reason)` with a structured reason enum
  (`unsupported_lang | no_tool | parse_error | timeout | ambiguous | private_or_internal_suspected |
  network_error | rate_limited | …`), provenance/tier, and a coverage record. Resolution-lane resolved value is
  `refuted` (never an asserted absence). One internal finding shape; SARIF 2.1.0 map at the edges (superset).
- The **fail-open recover/timeout harness** every backend runs inside (mirror `acli_subprocess`: process-group,
  SIGTERM→SIGKILL, structured timeout; a crash/timeout/missing-tool becomes a recorded `abstain`, never raises).
  The visible skip IS the coverage record — never a silent no-op.
- **Optional-extra scaffolding**: a new extra in `pyproject.toml`, `_optional.py` guard + `guard_import`, core
  stays import-clean (the existing optionality CI gate), non-adopting clients import nothing.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/` (new package) — contract + harness + extra wiring only; no backend logic.
- Polyglot/optional by construction; no Python-specific assumptions in the contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evidence model + structured reason enum + coverage implemented; `refute`/`abstain` representable; SARIF map both directions.
- [ ] Fail-open recover/timeout/subprocess harness: crash / timeout / missing tool → recorded `abstain`, never raises.
- [ ] Optional extra added; core import-clean (optionality CI gate passes); non-adopting client imports nothing.
- [ ] Unit-tested: harness converts each failure mode to the correct `abstain(reason)` + coverage entry.
data.title: Evidence contract + fail-open execution harness + optional-extra scaffolding (dependency root)
